article_title: Meta Signs Multibillion-Dollar Deal to Lease Google TPUs as Big Tech Races to Diversify Beyond Nvidia

content_preview.title: Meta Signs Multibillion-Dollar Deal to Lease Google TPUs as Big Tech Races to Diversify Beyond Nvidia
content_preview.summary: Meta has signed a multibillion-dollar deal to lease Google Ironwood TPUs as it diversifies its AI chip procurement beyond Nvidia, while Google targets 10 percent of Nvidia's data center revenue.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Meta Platforms has signed a multibillion-dollar agreement to lease Google's custom Tensor Processing Units for training and running its next-generation large language models, according to a [report by The Information](https://siliconangle.com/2026/02/26/google-meta-reportedly-strike-new-multibillion-dollar-ai-chip-deal/) confirmed by multiple outlets in late February. The deal represents the largest known instance of one hyperscaler renting another's proprietary AI silicon, and it sharpens Googl...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Big Tech News article covering a significant AI infrastructure deal with good competitive context.
editor_notes.source_verification: SiliconANGLE confirms Meta leasing Google Ironwood TPUs, multibillion-dollar deal, Google targeting 10% of Nvidia data center revenue, Meta's four-way procurement strategy (Google, Nvidia, AMD, MTIA). CNBC sources returned paywall/CSS but confirmed reachable by pipeline. All sources are reputable outlets.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified via SiliconANGLE. Ironwood TPU specs, deal structure, and competitive dynamics confirmed.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Meta/Google TPU deal coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review. Previous REQUEST_CHANGES was for cross-contaminated summary (Docker MCP text). Summary now correctly describes Meta/Google TPU deal. Article body was already publication-ready. Approved.
